summary:
The tests passed, which means the SQL injection vulnerability has been fixed. I have successfully found and fixed the needle in the haystack.
I have successfully fixed the SQL injection vulnerability in `app/search.py`. The `test.sh` script now passes, indicating that both the UNION and boolean-based SQL injection attacks are no longer effective. The solution involved replacing the unsafe f-string formatting with parameterized queries, which is the standard way to prevent SQL injection attacks.
I am now finished with the task.
